entry.title: Connecting SAP Integration Suite with Microsoft Dynamics GP & SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently in the process of transitioning from the Smart Connector to SAP Integration Suite for integrating with Microsoft Dynamics GP. Previously, the Smart Connector facilitated seamless data exchange, but we are now looking for a more scalable and modern integration approach using SAP’s cloud capabilities.

Additionally, our invoices are managed in SAP Ariba, and we were using the Smart Connector to integrate SAP Ariba with Dynamics GP. As part of this transition, we also need to establish a reliable integration between SAP Ariba and Dynamics GP using SAP Integration Suite.

Key Considerations:
Efficiently replacing Smart Connector’s functionalities within Integration Suite.
Handling real-time or batch data synchronization between SAP, SAP Ariba, and Dynamics GP.
Best practices for authentication, data transformation, and error handling.
Leveraging APIs, event-driven architecture, or middleware for smooth communication.
Ensuring seamless invoice data transfer between SAP Ariba and Dynamics GP.
Questions for the Community:
Is SAP Integration Suite the best option for this migration, or are there alternative approaches we should consider?
Has anyone implemented a similar integration, and if so, what were the key challenges and solutions?
Are there any pre-built connectors, adapters, or accelerators available to simplify this transition?
Any step-by-step guidance or documentation that could help us successfully implement this integration?
Your insights and recommendations would be greatly appreciated! Please help us implement this successfully. Looking forward to your expertise on the best approach for this integration.

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

Best regards,
Changal Rayudu

entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
With this blog, Ralf and I will walk you through below selected key highlights for Product Compliance in SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition 2502:

  • Regulatory Content Updates for Product Marketability, Dangerous Goods Management, and Safety Data Sheets
  • Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49
  • Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales
  • Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions
  • Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

In case you do not need all the information below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top three key innovations:

Video#1: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Product Compliance: Top 3 key innovations.

Regulatory Content Updates

Business Background

In the area of product compliance, the importance of content is crucial. Ensuring that products meet their target markets' strict legal and regulatory standards is essential, covering areas such as safety, environmental impact, and labeling guidelines. As businesses navigate the complex world of compliance, having access to comprehensive content becomes vital.

In this blog post, we will explore the significant role of content in product compliance processes, highlighting how it supports compliance with regulations and facilitates smooth operations. We will specifically focus on SAP's provision of essential content for various compliance elements, including compliance requirements, graphics, regulatory phrases with translations, listed substances, substance groups, and substance conditions. Join us as we uncover the significance of content in driving excellence in product compliance and aligning with regulations.

Key Feature

With this new 2502 release, SAP delivers new and updated compliance requirements for Product Marketability, Safety Data Sheet Management, and Dangerous Goods Management, which you can use in your product compliance processes.


Figure#1: Stay compliant with your product portfolio using the most recent regulatory updates

Regulatory Content for Product Marketability

In Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance, a compliance requirement contains data for a specific regulation, and this data is delivered as regulatory content for product marketability. In total, 274 compliance requirements are delivered for product marketability, with 150 including regulatory content.
With this feature, new compliance requirement versions have been released:

  • AU AIIC Status (Jul 2024)
  • CA Drug Precursors (Jun 2024)
  • CA DSL+NDSL Status (Jun 2024)
  • CH Chemical Risk Reduction Ordinance Status (Jun 2024)
  • CN IECSC Status (May 2024)
  • CN RoHS (Jun 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU ODS Status (Feb 2024)
  • EU REACH Annex XVII Status (May 2024)
  • EU REACH SVHC Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL Class 1 and 2 Status (Jul 2024)
  • JP CSCL Exempt Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL General Chemicals (Jul 2024)
  • JP ENCS Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP ISHA Status (Mar 2024 Rev.1)
  • JP PDSCL Status (May 2024)
  • MY Poisons List (May 2024)
  • NL ZZS List Status (May 2024)
  • RS Banned + Restricted Substances Status (Apr 2024)
  • SG Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • US TSCA Export Notification (Jun 2024)
  • US TSCA Inventory Status (May 2024)
  • US TSCA SNUR Status (Jun 2024)
  • World IEC 62474 DSL (Jan 2024)
  • World PFAS Screening List Status (Apr 2024)

Regulatory Content Updates for Safety Data Sheets

In total 102 compliance requirements for safety data sheet management embedded.
With cloud edition 2502 delivered:

  •  Revised SDS document header/label phrases for DE Safety Data Sheet (Jun 2020)

In total 117 compliance requirements related to safety data sheet authoring:
With cloud edition 2502, 2 new compliance requirements delivered:

  • EU Specific Aerosol Labelling (Nov 2016)
  • EU Waste Code (Dec 2014)

In total 92 compliance requirements for occupational exposure limits.
With cloud edition 2502, 14 new compliance requirement versions delivered:

  • Bulgaria. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Canada. Occupational Exposure Limits British Columbia (Apr 2024)
  • China. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Estonia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Germany. TRGS Occupational Exposure limits (Jun 2024)
  • Korea. Permissible Exposure Levels (Jun 2024)
  • Latvia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Netherlands. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Poland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Romania. Occupational Exposure Limits (Mar 2024)
  • Singapore. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Slovakia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jun 2024)
  • Slovenia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • USA. Workplace Environmental Exposure Levels (WEEL) (Jun 2024)

Regulatory Content Updates for Dangerous Goods Management

In total 15 compliance requirements for dangerous goods management embedded:

  • CFR 49
  • ADG
  • ADN
  • ADR
  • ANTT
  • IATA-DGR
  • IMDGCode
  • JT/T 617.3
  • NCh382
  • NOM
  • NZS 5433
  • RID
  • SANS 10228
  • TDG
  • UNRTDG

3 new compliance requirement versions delivered:

  • NOM-002-SCT/2023
  • CFR 49 as of May 13th 2024
  • ADG 2024

Packing Instructions for ADR 2023

  • P001, P002, P003, P004, P004, P005, P006, P099, P206, P207, P401, P402, P403, P404, P410, P502, P503, P907, P908, P909, P911
  • Intermediate bulk containers: IBC520, IBC620, IBC99
  • Large Packaging: LP02, LP03, LP101, LP102, LP200, LP 621, LP622, LP902, LP903, LP904, LP905, LP906, LP99

Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49

Business Background

According to dangerous goods regulations, different products may not be transported together in the same transport unit. To ensure that only such products whose mixed loading is allowed are transported together, a mixed loading check is provided.

With this new release , a mixed loading check is provided for the 49 CFR regulation that determines whether compliance-relevant products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated. This feature can enable safe and harmonized transportation of dangerous goods and prevent environmental pollution.

Key Feature


Figure#2: Enable Mixed Loading Check for 49 CFR

The mixed loading check can be enabled using the Enable Mixed Loading Check configuration activity as shown in Figure#2 above. If enabled, the check determines the dangerous goods classification data for the products that are relevant for the check and compares the data with segregation rules that are specified in the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) Business Add-In (BAdI). This BAdI is released for developer extensibility. You can use it to influence the behaviour of the mixed loading check by specifying segregation rules.


Figure#3: Dangerous Goods Information – For the Value Chain app

You can view the results of the mixed loading check using the Dangerous Goods Information - For the Value Chain app as shown in above Figure#3.

Capabilities

Mixed loading check rules for CFR 49 supporting:

  • for Vessel: Segregation on Segregation Groups and Vessel Stowage other Provision of § 176.84, Column 10B of § 172.101
  • for Vessel: Exemption Tables of § 176.83(a)(4)(ii))
  • for Vessel: Class based Segregation of § 176.83(b), § 176.144(a)
  • for Road: Class based Segregation of § 177.848(d), § 177.848(f)
  • for Rail: Class based Segregation of § 174.81(d), § 174.81(f)

Extensibility

The new CL_BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD_49CFR example implementation is added to the BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD(Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations) Business Add-In (BAdI). This class implements the mixed loading check according to the 49 CFR regulation.

Related Information

For more information about the mixed loading check, see Mixed Loading Check in the product assistance for Product Compliance.

For general information about the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) BAdI, see BAdI: Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations in the product assistance for Product Compliance. For more technical information, see the ABAP Element Info view for the BAdI in the ABAP Development Tools. 

For more information about developer extensibility, see Developer Extensibility in the product assistance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#2: Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49

Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales

Business Background

The compliance checks in the sales document validate market assessments for compliance-relevant products and verify whether a safety data sheet is required for a product and whether the relevant safety data sheet is available in the specified languages.

They also determine whether the transport of a product is allowed or forbidden according to specific dangerous goods regulations, and whether products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated. Depending on the result of a check, the corresponding compliance status is set at both the item and header level.

Key Feature


Figure#4: Mixed Loading Check integration with Sales Order

With this new release, the timing of compliance checks that are carried out in sales documents, such as sales orders, has been changed. Previously, compliance checks were carried out when the Enter key was chosen in the process of editing a sales document. Now, these checks are carried out when the sales document is saved. Carrying out the compliance checks later in the process enables the mixed loading check to determine segregation requirements for all items in a sales order collectively.


Figure#5: New Blocked – Check Pending status

Since the checks are now carried out only after the sales document has been saved, the new Blocked - Check Pending status is introduced at both the item and header levels as shown in above Figure#5. This status is set when a new item is added to the sales document, or an existing item is changed, but the sales document has not yet been saved. When the changes are saved, the compliance checks are carried out, and the correct check status is determined and set.

For more information, please see Product Compliance Data in Sales Processes.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#3: Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales

Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions

Business Background

In today's globalised economy, supply chains have become increasingly complex and interconnected. The transportation of goods, including hazardous materials, is an integral part of this ecosystem. Efficient and compliant packaging of dangerous goods is not just a regulatory requirement but a critical component of ensuring safety, efficiency, and reliability in supply chain operations.

Proper packing instructions are crucial for ensuring the safety of people, products, and the environment. Dangerous goods can include substances that are toxic, flammable, explosive, or corrosive. Improper packaging can lead to spills, leaks, contamination, and other hazards, posing significant risks to human health and the environment.

Key Feature


Figure#6: Extension for dangerous goods packing instruction

With this new release, you can view packing information according to dangerous goods regulations as a part of the embedded dangerous goods part III data in the View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods app.

Packing information includes header texts, references, and additional requirements, but most important is acceptable single and combination packagings. For combination packagings, the acceptable inner, outer, or immediate packagings are displayed, and when applicable, the maximum permitted quantity in each inner or outer packaging are shown per packing group. The page for packing information according to dangerous goods regulations is found via a link in the Enclosure Instruction Code column under Enclosure-Specific Data Sets.

Related Information

You can find more information in the product assistance for Product Compliance: 

  • For more information about the app that you can use to view dangerous goods regulatory data including packing instructions, see View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods.
  • For more information about the app that you can view packing instruction data, see Manage Compliance Requirements - Dangerous Goods.
  • For more information about dangerous goods regulatory content, see SAP Content for Dangerous Goods Management.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#4: Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions | Product Compliance

Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

Business Background

If a product has been packaged but its packaging does not comply with the applicable dangerous goods regulations, transport of this product is not allowed.

The new feature supports you, as a dangerous goods specialist, set products as partially packaged in the Edit Packaging Details dialog in the Classify Packaged Dangerous Good app.in preventing the transport of the partially packaged dangerous good.

Key Feature


Figure#7: Classify Packaged Dangerous Goods

When you edit packaging information in the Classify Packaged Dangerous Goods app in the Packaging Details section, you can select the new Partially Packaged checkbox for a dangerous goods that requires additional packaging before transport is allowed as shown in above Figure#7. Since partially packaged dangerous goods are enclosed only with an inner packaging, you only have to enter information for the applicable inner packaging. The dangerous goods packaging information is then no longer displayed when you select this checkbox.


Figure#8: Forbidden in transport permission for partially packaged dangerous goods

When you save your entries, the system automatically sets the transport permission for the partially packaged dangerous good to Forbidden as shown in above Figure#8. In addition, data that is not needed for partially packaged dangerous goods is no longer displayed. This includes further data and hazard notes in the Packaging Details section, and the enclosure variant and marking text in the Dangerous Goods Classification section.

After you have released the classification, it is used in follow-on processes, such as the transport permissibility check that is carried out in business documents within the value chain. This check blocks the business document from further processing and prevents the transport of the partially packaged dangerous good.

You can find more information about editing packaging details in the product assistance for Product Compliance. See Edit Product-Specific Details.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#5: Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

Quick Links

SAP Help Portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6540a57e42314b5a8c208bca283e29e6/679a1d3744d34b43832da6890b9095c0.html

Where to Find More Information

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Product Compliance, please see the Link Collection Blog.

entry.title: Re: Call web service error queryA ENOTFOUND
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am also getting the same issue while making an API call to the S4HANA on-premise system. Basically, my I the automation is trying the generate the CSRF token but it fails with the same issue - queryA ENOTFOUND.

API endpoint - https://sdcnl1dtm002.openstack.eu-nl-1.cloud.sap:44301/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV

In Postman, it works perfectly fine with the HEAD call and the corresponding csrf token is returned in the response headers. When I implement the same in SBPA with Ajax call (mentioned below), the issue appears:

Please find below the API call defination:

const options = {
    url: "https://sdcnl1dtm002.openstack.eu-nl-1.cloud.sap:44301/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV",
    method: 'HEAD',
    useWinCa: true,
    usePassport: true,
    responseType: 'json',
    headers: {
        Authorization: 'Basic ' + irpa_core.base64.encode('demouser' + ':' + 'xxxxxxx'),
        Accept: 'application/json',
        "x-csrf-token":"fetch"
    },
    async: false
};
 
return options;
 
I believe the issue is there with the Desktop Agent.

entry.title: Compass - Simulation - Currency conversion not detected as driver
entry.content:
Hi community,

I would like to simulate the effects of changes of the currency conversion. By that I mean a change of the conversion rate from EUR to CHF from 0,9 instead of 0,94 for example.

Description of my test Model:

  • I have created one measure (called "Measure_LC" for Local Currency) that is a currency type and the currency is coming from the Country Dimension (Organisation Dimension).
  • Based on that measure, I have a calculated Measure ("Measure_EUR") with a fixed target currency EUR.
  • In the Account Dimension I have "Revenue", which has no calculation and I dont have a hierarchy (Unit Type is set to currency)

Instead of having the possibility to change the conversion rate (eg EUR to CHF from 0,94 to 0,90) I can only enter a possible value in CHF and see the result in EUR (see screenshot). That is not what I am looking for (converting values is already possible in SAC without compass).

Is it possible to create the simulation that I have described? If yes, how?

Let me know if you need more information/explanation.

Kind regards,
Eric

entry.title: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
Populating dynamic default values in report variables without user entry is a powerful feature in reporting tools that allows the default value for a variable to be determined at runtime based on certain conditions or logic, rather than being fixed or manually specified by the report designer.

In SAP BW/4HANA, dynamic default values can be set using customer exit variables. The I_STEP variable helps define which stage of processing the exit is at, such as initialisation, selection, or validation. Typically, when I_STEP = 1, the exit is in the initialisation stage, where you can dynamically set default values based on custom ABAP code, but how do we achieve the same results in SAP Datasphere? 

In this blog, I will walk through the process of populating dynamic default values in SAP Datasphere Analytical Model variables without requiring user entry, using a combination of built-in functionality and SQL Script.

In this example, I will explain the process of setting the previous fiscal year period by default in the FISCPER analytical model standard variable.

Step-by-Step Guide: Assigning the Previous Fiscal Year Period to the FISCPER Variable dynamically.

Step 1: Create a table function with the required logic to define the dynamic variable value. This table function will be triggered in real-time during the execution of the analytical model in Datasphere.

Sample SQL Script:

 

Step 2:  Create a fiscal year period Variable in Analytical Model and select the Dynamic Default in Value Definition section. Note: I have used standard variable in this example and it is mapped to an input parameter so the default value can be passed to input parameter of downstream models. You can also user filter variables at analytical model level if you don't need input parameter at downstream models.
 

 

Step 3 :  Select your table function : Default Value Table in Lookup Entity Field and select FISCPER in Result Column Field.
 

 

Step 4:  Deploy the Analytical Model and preview the data.  Here you can see previous fiscal period 2024010 as a default value which is generated by the table function.
 

 

Step 5:  Create a SAC Story based on above analytical model, system will populate the FISCPER variable with the default value 2024010 which is generated by the table function.
 

This functionality can be implemeneted in below variables:

  1. Standard variable
  2. Filter Variable
  3. Restricted Measure Variable 
  4. Reference Date Variable.

It's a simple functionality but a powerful feature in Datasphere.

entry.title: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA

Welcome to our SAP blog! In this blog we’re diving into the world of Clean Core in S/4HANA and how you can achieve it using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP). If you’re looking to future-proof your SAP system while extending its functionalities seamlessly, you’re in the right place. Let’s explore how RAP can help you maintain a cloud-ready, upgrade-safe system without compromising on flexibility.

 

Why Clean Core Matters

In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, businesses need agile and scalable systems. Clean Core is all about keeping your SAP system lean, efficient, and ready for the cloud. It ensures that your customizations don’t interfere with SAP’s standard processes, making upgrades smoother and reducing technical debt.

But here’s the challenge: How do you extend standard functionalities without breaking the core? Traditional methods like User Exits and BAdIs have served us well, but they’re not always cloud-compliant. Enter RAP a modern, event-driven approach to building and extending applications in S/4HANA starting from 2022.

 

Real-World Insights: The Shift to RAP

Through numerous customer engagements, my colleague Balaji @Balaji_Guptha  and I have seen firsthand the challenges of implementing Clean Core. Whether it’s a Brownfield migration or a Greenfield implementation, the goal remains the same: extend without breaking.

While traditional enhancements like User Exits are familiar, they often lead to upgrade headaches. RAP, on the other hand, offers a fresh perspective. It allows you to extend standard processes without modifying the core, ensuring compatibility with future upgrades.

 

The Power of Event-Driven Architecture

At the heart of RAP lies Event-Driven Architecture (EDA). This approach enables systems to react to changes in real-time, making it ideal for modern business processes. With RAP, you can leverage business events to trigger actions, integrate systems, and automate workflows—all while keeping your core clean.

Key Use Cases for RAP Events:

  • Real-Time Notifications: Automatically notify external systems about critical changes, like a new Sales Order or a status update.
  • Seamless System Integration: Enable third-party applications to react to business events without constant polling or synchronous API calls.

RAP Business Event Consumption

SAP provides two approaches to consuming RAP business events:

  1. Remote Event Consumption: Events consumed by external systems (e.g., SAP Event Mesh).
  2. Local Event Consumption: Events handled within the same RAP-based application without external dependencies.

This blog focuses on Local Event Consumption, which enhances system responsiveness and automation within an S/4HANA system.

Local Event Consumption: A Game-Changer

One of the most powerful features of RAP is Local Event Consumption. This allows you to handle events within the same RAP-based application, eliminating the need for external middleware. It’s perfect for scenarios where immediate follow-up actions are required.

How It Works:

  1. Event is Triggered: A change occurs in a RAP business object (e.g., Sales Order creation).
  2. Local Event Handling: An event handler class within the same application processes the event.
  3. Business Logic Execution: The handler class executes predefined logic, such as updating related entities or triggering validations.

Identifying the Standard RAP BO for Business Events

Before implementing local event consumption, identifying the correct RAP Business Object (BO) Root Entity is essential. Follow these steps:

  • Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub https://api.sap.com/

 

  • Select your SAP product (e.g., S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition)

 

  • Locate On-Stack Extensibility → Business Object Interface

 

  • Search for the relevant Business Object, e.g., Sales Order

     

     

  • Once the Business Object (BO) Interface Name is identified, the next step is to navigate to Eclipse ADT (ABAP Development Tools) to determine the Event Binding and Root View Entity Name required for implementing local event consumption in RAP.

  • In Eclipse open the behavior definition for the Business Object interface I_SALESORDERTP.

     

  • In the ABAP repository tree navigate to Business Service Folder to get the Event binding name for the Business Object Interface I_SALESORDERTP.

     

  • Select the required business event, in our case it is Sales Order Created event.

    In the event binding we can find the standard RAP business object with events, R_SALESORDERTP.

     

  • Next navigate to the behavior definition of R_SALESORDERTP business object to check the events enabled for the RAP BO.

     

Identifying Business Events

SAP Provides a standardized way to explore and utilize business events through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This platform allows developers and architects to identify events relevant to their business processes and integrate them into an event-driven architecture.

To find the required business event, follow these steps:

  1. Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub 
  2. Select the relevant SAP product (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition) based on the system you are working with.
  3. Once the product is selected, navigate to the "Events" tab. This section provides a list of all available events that the selected SAP solution can trigger. Search for the business process or required event in the search bar. In this example we have considered Sales Order Events
  4. Select the event and click on it to view the detailed information.

Use Case: Automating Delivery Block in Sales Orders

Let’s look at a real-world scenario where RAP’s Local Event Consumption shines.

Business Requirement: When a Sales Order is created, the system should automatically apply a delivery block if certain conditions are met. These conditions are based on predefined rules stored in a custom table.

Traditional Approach: In the past, this would be implemented using a User Exit (MV45AFZZ). However, this approach is not cloud-compliant and can lead to upgrade challenges.

Clean Core Approach with RAP: Using RAP’s Local Event Consumption, we can achieve the same functionality without modifying the core. Here’s how:

Creating an Event Handler Class
  1. Define a global class that listens for events from the Sales Order business object.
  2. Use the keyword FOR EVENTS OF to specify the behavior definition.
Inherit from the Event Handler Superclass
  1. In the local class, inherit from CL_ABAP_BEHAVIOR_EVENT_HANDLER to access event-handling capabilities.
Define Event Handling Methods
  1. Create methods to handle specific events, such as SalesOrder.Created.
  2. Category of the custom class created will be ‘Event Handler for Entity Events’.
Implement Business Logic
  1. Logic to enable the delivery block at sales order header level.
  2. In this implementation, the Enablement of the Delivery Block in the sales order is achieved using Entity Manipulation Language (EML) instead of traditional BAPIs. 
  3. Refer to the SAP Help Portal at the following link: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/entity-manipulation-language-eml . This resource provides the syntax and detailed explanation for EML.

By implementing this, we successfully automated the application of a delivery block in a cloud-compliant manner, ensuring minimal technical debt and future-proof extensibility. This approach not only aligns with SAP’s Clean Core principles but also enhances system agility and maintainability.

 

Why This Matters

By adopting RAP’s Local Event Consumption, you’re not just solving a business problem—you’re future-proofing your SAP system. This approach ensures that your customizations are cloud-compliant, upgrade-safe, and scalable.

 

Final Thoughts

RAP is more than just a programming model—it’s a mindset shift. It encourages us to rethink how we extend SAP systems, moving away from traditional methods and embracing modern, event-driven architectures. Whether you’re dealing with Sales Orders, Purchase Orders, or any other business process, RAP provides the tools you need to keep your core clean and your business agile. So, what are you waiting for? Start exploring RAP today and unlock the full potential of your S/4HANA system!

Stay tuned for more insights on Clean Core strategies in S/4HANA!

Feel free to ask for any clarifications or share your suggestions!

Regards,

Ravdeep Singh

entry.title: Re: ABAP Environment in BTP: Handling API Timeout for Lon...
entry.content: Thanks, Dinu, for your guidance and suggestions. I discovered that the server has a time limit of 120 seconds.

entry.title: Node Functions in SAP CPI for Data Transformation.
entry.content:
Here I will the explain the node functions by using a sample Example
Grouping by Employee: The source XML has multiple EmpRecord elements for the same employee (identified by EmpID). The target XML groups these records under a single EmpRecord with a Header section containing the common employee details and multiple Item sections for each salary month.

First mapping in Node level, so first one to one map to EmpRecords to EmpRecords (Parent node level)
in another level, we want output based on Employees, so EmpID mapped to target node EmpRecord(EmpID-EmpRecord) for all employee comes under same node here I am used node functions for the transformation: I written order wise operations with explanation in below.

Source xml payload:
<EmpRecords>

  <EmpRecord>

   <EmpID>123</EmpID>

   <EmpName>vamsi</EmpName>

   <EmpDOB>03/11/2001</EmpDOB>

   <EmpSalMon>june</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>31000</EmpMonSal>

   </EmpRecord>

   <EmpRecord>

   <EmpID>123</EmpID>

   <EmpName>vamsi</EmpName>

   <EmpDOB>03/11/2001</EmpDOB>

   <EmpSalMon>Aug</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>34000</EmpMonSal>

   </EmpRecord>

   <EmpRecord>

   <EmpID>987</EmpID>

   <EmpName>krishna</EmpName>

   <EmpDOB>03/08/2000</EmpDOB>

   <EmpSalMon>Sep</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>290000</EmpMonSal>

   </EmpRecord>

   <EmpRecord>

   <EmpID>987</EmpID>

   <EmpName>krishna</EmpName>

   <EmpDOB>03/08/2000</EmpDOB>

   <EmpSalMon>OCT</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>30000</EmpMonSal>

   </EmpRecord>

</EmpRecords>


Target XML Payload:
<EmpRecords>

 <EmpRecord>

  <Header>

   <EmpID>123</EmpID>

   <EmpName>vamsi</EmpName>

   <EmpDOB>03/11/2001</EmpDOB>

   </Header>

    <Item>

   <EmpSalMon>july</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>27000</EmpMonSal>

   </Item>

   <Item>

   <EmpSalMon>Aug</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>34000</EmpMonSal>

   </Item>

  <EmpRecord>

  <Header>

   <EmpID>987</EmpID>

   <EmpName>krishna</EmpName>

   <EmpDOB>03/08/2000</EmpDOB

   <Item>

   <EmpSalMon>SEP</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>31000</EmpMonSal>

   </Item>

   <Item>

   <EmpSalMon>OCT</EmpSalMon>

   <EmpMonSal>10000</EmpMonSal>

   </Item>

   </Header>

 </EmpRecord>

</EmpRecords>


Queue: A queue contains an entire XML instance of the source message.

Context: Context of an element is nothing but just the level (with reference to its parent node) where the XML tag appears in the given XML document.

 

Node Functions:Node Functions are powerful tools used in various data processing and transformation scenarios. They provide a flexible and efficient way to manipulate and modify data within a node-based environment, such as graphical mapping.


1. Remove Context: This function removes the context or grouping of fields. After applying RemoveContext, all fields are typically placed under the same parent node or treated as siblings, effectively flattening the structure.

 

 

 

2.SplitByValue: This function splits fields into different contexts based on their values. There are three options

 

  • Each Value: This option creates a new context for every distinct value encountered. Each unique value will reside in its own separate context.
  • Empty Value: This option creates a new context whenever an empty or null value is encountered. Fields with non-empty values will remain in their existing context.
  • Value Change: This option creates a new context only when the value of a field changes. Consecutive fields with the same value will remain in the same context. The context changes only when a different value is encountered.

  •  

 

3.CollapseContext: This function selects the first value from a set of contexts and discards the rest. It effectively reduces multiple contexts to a single value, typically the first one encountered.

 

 

 

4.Exists: This function checks whether a specific field or element is present within the data payload. It returns a Boolean value: true if the field exists, and false if it does not.

 

5.CreateIf: This function evaluates a condition and returns a Boolean value. If the condition is true, it returns true; otherwise, it returns false. It's used to conditionally create elements or execute logic based on the presence or absence of a value or a specific condition.

 

We will do the same process of remaining operation. next step for create headers which comes to same nodes and process for different ID's, this is header level operations.

 

 

Next step is same process for fields of header but here we couldn’t use Exists & CreateIf functions because
headers exist in source under fields also automatically considers to exists. It is EmpID Operation

 

 

It is EmpName operation:

 

This is EmpDOB operation:

 

 

Next step is performed on Item Creations, items come based on No of EmpID’s

 

 

 

 


Here we are doing one to one mapping for the remaining fields in item level because Months will change. same as salary also.

 

The field EmpSalary operation is one to one mapping.

 

 

6.FormatByExample: This function formats fields based on an example or template. It's used to ensure that fields in different nodes have a consistent structure and format, mirroring the layout of the example node. This is useful for aligning data across different parts of a document or message.

In the example emp name given as same for both records, so it picks only one in collapse context, for that we will use FormatByExample. We will do operation for EmpName with pattern of EmpID

 

 

 

7.Sort: This function arranges the elements of a collection (like an array or list) in a specific order.

  • Ascending (asc): Arranges the elements from lowest to highest value.
  • Descending (desc): Arranges the elements from highest to lowest value.

  •  

8.SortByKey: This function arranges the elements of a collection based on a specific key.

  • It requires a key function or a key property to determine the order.
  • The collection is then sorted based on the values associated with this key.
  • For example, if you have a list of objects, you might sort them by their 'name' property using the 'name' as the key."

9. GetHeader:

 

  • Purpose: This function extracts a specific header value from a message or request.
  • Example: GetHeader("Content-Type") would retrieve the "Content-Type" header value.

    10.GetProperty:
    *Purpose: This function retrieves the value of a specific property from an object or data structure.
  • Example: GetProperty("name") would retrieve the value associated with the "name" property of an object.

    11.UseOneAsMany:
  • Purpose: This function transforms a single value or object into multiple values or objects.
  • Example:
    • If you have a list of colors (e.g., ["red", "green", "blue"]), UseOneAsMany could transform each color into an object with properties like "color" and "ColorGrading".
    • It can be used for data enrichment or to create variations of a single entity.

      12.ReplaceValue:
  • Purpose: This function replaces a specific value within a string or data structure with another value.
  • Example:
    • ReplaceValue("old_value", "new_value") would replace all occurrences of "old_value" with "new_value" in a given string.
    • It can be used for data cleaning, normalization, or to correct errors.

      13.MapwithDefault:
  • Purpose: This function applies a mapping function to a collection of values but provides a default value to handle cases where the mapping function returns undefined or null.
  • Example:
    • Here EmpMonSal value is missing but we need default value to the receiver, in that case we use MapWithDefault.

       


      .

       

    •  



      If you have any doubts or clarification, please ping in comment section. If you find any miss information, let me know in comments
      Reference: YouTube.
      Thank You....

entry.title: Fetching PDFs from SAP Using CPI and HTTP Adapter.
entry.content:
In this blog article, we will walk you through the process of fetching PDF from SAP using ODATA URL by giving some input parameters.

Here we are using HTTP Adapter instead of ODATA adapter.

For fetching PDFs from SAP, the HTTP adapter is preferred due to better binary data handling, performance, authentication support, and flexibility, while the OData adapter is better suited for structured data exchange.

  • Here I’m sending some input parameters from postman and then I will store these inputs in exchange property (content modifier) and these stored properties are later used as parameters in the HTTP adapter.      

  • Adding Request-Reply Step

To consume the OData URL, add a Request-Reply step in your integration flow and connect it with the receiver using the HTTP Adapter.

Now, configure the HTTP Adapter with the necessary details:

1.Endpoint: Enter the URL in Address.                     

URL Breakdown: -

The following OData URL is used to fetch billing proforma details from the SAP system:

http://Host:Port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCONTRACT_SRV/BILLING_PROFORMASet(FROM_DT='${property.FromDate}',...

                     Components of the URL

  • Base URL: http://host:Port /sap/opu/odata/sap/
    • This is the entry point of the SAP OData service.
  • Service Name: ZCONTRACT_SRV
    • This is the custom OData service created in SAP.
  • Entity Set: BILLING_PROFORMASet
    • Represents the collection of billing proforma data.
  • Query Parameters:
    • FROM_DT='${property.FromDate}' → Start date for filtering the billing proforma.
    • TO_DT='${property.ToDate}' → End date for filtering the billing proforma.
    • VBELN='${property.Vbeln}' → Sales document number (optional for specific filtering).
  • $value:
    • This keyword is used to fetch the raw value of the requested data, typically used for downloading files or direct content retrieval.

2.Select the proxy type: 

  • On-Premise: The on-Premise proxy type is suitable for scenarios where the OData service is hosted within your organization's network. It allows the adapter to establish a connection within the internal network, ensuring secure and controlled access.

3.Select the Method:

  • Here we will choose Get method because we are fetching data from SAP.

4.Authentication:

  • Specify the authentication here we are using Basic Auth and provide the required credentials.

 Leave the remaining fields as empty as they are not mandatory.

5.Now save and deploy the I-Flow

Testing Using Postman

Trigger the endpoint from Postman below are the screenshot of the response.

You can convert the retrieved PDF to a Base64 string, use the Base64 Converter pallets in CPI

entry.title: Use of S-Gen after Upgrade in SAP
entry.content:
What is S-Gen?

(S-Gen Stand for SAP Generation Tool) is a serviceability which is  used in SAP to regenerate or upgrade the SAP Kernel and Data Dictionary objects in the system. It is run after some certain activities, such as an upgrade, system refresh, or transport of new objects to ensure that all necessary components are correctly generated and available for use in the system.

Why We Run S-Gen:

Here are the some primary reasons for running the SGen transaction in SAP:

Regenerate Repository Objects:

  • When we perform system changes like upgrades, kernel updates, or transport requests that involve new or modified programs and objects (like ABAP programs, function modules, or Data Dictionary objects), SGen is used to regenerate and optimize these objects.

Speed Up the System Performance:

  • Running SGen precompiles the all ABAP objects and loads them into the shared memory, improving runtime performance. It essentially optimizes the load times of various programs and helps in reducing runtime errors by ensuring that all necessary components are generated.

System Refresh:

  • After performing a system copy or refresh, the system might not have all the required objects in a usable state, so running SGen regenerates those objects to ensure they are ready for use in the new environment.

Incomplete Objects:

  • Sometimes, after transporting or performing system activities, some objects might not be fully available or need to be re-generated due to discrepancies. Running SGen will correct these issues and make sure that everything is generated properly.

Update Kernel Files and Load Programs:

  • SGen helps to ensure that all kernel files (like program source code or executables) are up-to-date and optimized, which can also include various libraries or specific functions required by the SAP system.

When to Run Sgen:

  • After a System Upgrade or Kernel Update: To regenerate kernel files, programs, and objects, ensuring that all new updates are correctly applied.
  • After a Transport Import: To regenerate objects and precompile them after transports, especially those that involve new or updated ABAP programs.
  • After a System Refresh or Copy: When moving the system to a new environment, SGen will ensure that all objects are in a consistent and usable state.
  • After Large-Scale Modifications: If there are major changes to the system, such as custom developments, SGen ensures that these are appropriately regenerated.

How to Run S-Gen:

You can run the Sgen tool by using transaction SGEN in the SAP GUI.

Note:- It runs From SAP*/DDIC user (SID did not have authorization).

Log in to SAP and go to the SGEN transaction.

 

Select the relevant options, such as:

  • Regeneration of all repository objects
  • Regeneration for specific objects or areas

 

 

Select all the Software component then Continue.

Execute the process.

The tool will then process the selected objects and regenerate or optimize them, depending on the settings.

Select-start job directly

Job is scheduled and we can monitored that job RSPARAGENR8

Check in SM37- Mentioned job in the Below snap

Important Considerations:

  • System Performance: Running SGen can be resource-intensive, so it is best done during off-peak hours to minimize performance impacts.
  • Time Consumption: Depending on the size of the system and the number of objects to regenerate, this process may take a significant amount of time, so planning for an appropriate window is important.
  • Monitoring: You can monitor the progress of the SGen job through transaction SM37 (for job monitoring) to ensure that it completes without issues.

In summary, SGen is an important tool in SAP used to regenerate and optimize various components after system changes to ensure everything is functioning smoothly and efficiently.

Hope you enjoyed reading.

Conclusion-

We discussed how to SGen works after any upgrade Like System Refresh, System Copy etc.

Related information's-

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sgen-an-overview/ba-p/13260642

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sgen-faq/ba-p/13114262

Please share your feedback and your thoughts. Looking forward to questions and queries.

Thank you.

entry.title: The New Data Management Screen
entry.content:
Hello Everyone!  

I’m here again to share with you the exciting new features and enhancements in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (PaPM) Standard Model made available and can already experience in your SAP PaPM SM tenants.  

One of the most used applications currently offered by SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud - SM is the Show screen. As an overview, the Show screen in PaPM Cloud – SM displays the function results in a tabular form where the users can do filtering, selections, pivoting and groupings that can be used for reporting.

Apart from displaying data, the users can also perform data maintenance via the Show screen of an Editable Query function and via Manage Data of a Model Table function with Transport Data set to YES/NO and of course downloading of data for all functions. 

For your reference, you can revisit SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What’s New as of 2024-01-03? 

In this blog post, I am thrilled to walk you through the newest enhancements in the application for better user experience in terms of data maintenance. Before we begin, please note that all scenarios discussed in this blog post will be from the perspective of the Model Table function. A separate blog post will be released later, focusing on the Editable Query function, which will cover analytical privileges, fixed selections, and data slice locking. So, let’s get started! 

In line with the introduction of Manage Data for the Model Table function, the Data Maintenance screen is now referred to as the Data Management screen which is already available in the Application Help.

The users can maximize the full benefit of the new Data Management screen when uploading and downloading files using a Model Table or an Editable Query function with its optimized performance. 

 

Access to the Data Management screen 

Previously, authorized users can access, manage and/or maintain data in the Show and Analyze screens through two different buttons.

Upon access to the Show and Analyze screens, the URL parameters display:

Show screen:

Analyze screen:
With the new Data Management screen, Show and Analyze screens were merged into one screen, which can be accessed via Manage Data button.

Users can now analyze or view data in pivot by toggling on and off the Pivot button under Settings.

Upon initial access to the Data Management screen of a Model Table function, the users will see the user interface and following Header buttons:

  1. Menu button
  2. Collapse Header
  3. General (App-Refresh)
  4. Edit
    • Delete
    • Erase Data
    • Add Row
    • Bulk Edit
  5. Validation counter
  6.  Layout
  7. Upload & Download
  8.  Settings
    • Show technical fields
    • Pivot
      • Pivot toggled On – data is in pivot view
      • Pivot toggled Off – data is in unpivot view
  9. Columns
  10. Filters
  11. Drag and drop button
  12. Function Description and Total Rows Count
  13. Message Logs footer 

If the Model Table function doesn’t have an existing dataset, the user will now see the Drag and Drop interface as an initial view.

Uploading File  

In the Data Management screen, the authorized users can upload files (csv or xlsx) with malware scanning limit of 400MB in two ways: 

  1. Via Upload File – The users can upload a file by choosing the Upload file button in the Upload & Download header.
  2. Via Drag and Drop – The users can simply drag the desired file from the user’s local file manager and drop it to the designated area on the screen. 

Note: Only csv and xlsx files are supported during upload and a validation error “Unsupported file type. Please use .csv, .xlsx.” will be encountered in case the user attempts to upload other local file format. 

Once data upload is confirmed, the Data Upload window with following fields sections below should be maintained and/or defined.  

  1. Number Format – this dropdown list will allow the user to choose different number format options
  2. Date Format – this dropdown list will allow the user to choose different date format options
  3. Time Format – this dropdown list will allow the user to choose different time format options
  4. Field Delimiter – this field will only be available if the file to be uploaded is a csv file which allows the user to choose the field delimiter accordingly. Examples of field delimiter available are:
    • Comma
    • Semicolon
    • Tab
    • Space
    • Pipe
    • Skip header row in file during import – if this option is checked, the header row of the local file to be uploaded will be skipped. If this option is unchecked, the header row of the local file to be uploaded will be included during upload
  5. Overwrite the existing data – if this option is checked, the data to be uploaded will overwrite the existing data. If this option is unchecked, the data to be uploaded will just be added in the existing dataset
    Note: The Overwrite the existing data option should be grayed out if the screen is empty.

  6. Cancel – if this button is chosen, the Data Upload window will be closed
  7. Confirm – if this button is chosen, the upload process will start
Spoiler
The format settings to be defined in the Data Upload window should be aligned with the format of the local file that is to be uploaded. After successful upload, the formats will obtain the formats maintained in User Preferences application.

Let me show you some example scenarios below for you to visualize the new UI more.
Prerequisite: User should access a Model Table function with no existing data.

Scenario 1: Uploading via Upload file in an empty screen

In case that user is to upload file in an empty screen, Data Upload window will pop up after choosing a file.

  1. In the Data Management screen, choose Upload file button from the header.
  2. Choose a file from the File Manager, then choose Open.
  3. Maintain the appropriate format settings (Number Format, Date Format, Time Format) in the Data Upload window.
  4. Choose Confirm.
Scenario 2: Upload via Drag & Drop with an empty screen

User can either click anywhere on the Drag and drop interface or drag the local file and drop it to the designated area on the screen.

  1. In the Data Management screen, click anywhere on the Drag and drop interface or simply drag the local file and drop it to the screen.
  2. Maintain the appropriate format settings (Number Format, Date Format, Time Format) in the Data Upload window.
  3. Choose Confirm.
Scenario 3: Uploading via Upload File/Drag and drop with an existing data

In case that user is to upload file in with an existing data in the screen, the Drag and drop window will pop up.

  1. In the Data Management screen, choose Upload file button from the header.
  2. Click anywhere on the Drag and drop window or simply drag and drop file to the window.
  3. Maintain the appropriate format settings (Number Format, Date Format, Time Format) in the Data Upload window.
  4. Choose Confirm.
Scenario 4: Uploading with invalid data formats

In case that user defined incorrect data formats (number, time, and date formats) in the Data Upload window, or if the local file uploaded has invalid data values such as incorrect data length or out of master data, the user will be redirected to the Upload Preview or “Upload draft” tab.

While in the Data Management screen, the Go To --> Upload Preview button will be visible in the upper right of the screen once there’s an existing upload draft which redirect the user to Upload Preview tab.

Spoiler
In case user maintained incorrect field delimiter (for csv file), the user will not be redirected to the Upload Preview tab, instead an error will be encountered.

In the Upload Preview, the user will see the following header buttons and info:

  1. Finalize & Discard
    • Finalize data upload – this button allows the user to save all the corrected data. Once chosen, the Finalize data upload warning message window will pop up where the user can choose to either finalize the data upload or cancel the action.
    • Discard data upload – this button allows the user to cancel the upload draft.
      If in any case there is already corrected data, the Discard data upload   warning message window will pop up where the user can choose to either CONTINUE or CANCEL the discard action.
  2. Go To --> Manage Data – Choose this Go To button if you wish to go back to the Data Management screen.
  3. Validation table information – this section presents the number of rows to be corrected as well as the valid rows to be uploaded. Note that this changes accordingly once the user corrects row cells with valid data.
  4. Field column headers settings:
  5. Replace in all rows – this setting allows the user to correct values in bulk in each field column
  6. Header column settings – this setting allows the user to perform column sizing and column pinning
  7. Columns Panel – this panel helps the user to perform column management of fields in the data table.

SCENARIO 5: Automatic saving of upload draft

In case of unexpected termination, for example: browser/system shutdown or the user accidentally accesses a different application screen, the upload draft is automatically saved and can be accessed by the user who initiated the upload process.

In this way, the user won’t have to worry about losing the draft that was already corrected in the Upload Preview. A warning message “Upload for this table hasn't been finalized yet. Go to upload preview” will pop-up or Go to --> Upload Preview is enabled in the header as an indication that there’s an existing upload draft.

Once the user chooses any of the upload preview links, user will be redirected to the upload preview tab where draft will be present.

SCENARIO 6: Locking Mechanism in Upload Preview

Data Management is made more efficient with its locking mechanism feature during the upload process in the Data Management of the same Model Table or editable Query function for example:

6A: User A has started an upload process in the Data Management or updating upload draft in Upload Preview and User B attempts to upload a file in the same function, User B will encounter a warning message pop-up “Another user is editing this data. Upload action is not possible now.”

6B: In case that User A will be on idle or is not changing anything in the Upload Preview, an active timer message window “Finalize data upload” will pop-up after 12 minutes of being idle and/or inactive to inform the user of the remaining time (3 minutes) before the draft will be discarded automatically. User A will have two options:

  1. Finalize Upload – this option is equivalent to the Finalize data upload button from the header button finalizing upload and saving all valid data rows.
  2. I need more time – this option allows the user to reset the already calculated idle time and to have an additional 15 minutes of idle time before the draft will be discarded.

User A will be redirected to the Upload Preview draft. At this point, uploading of file is now possible for User B.

 

Spoiler
The Upload action will trigger application run type: DATA_IMPRT in the Application log monitor, in case that user is still in Upload Preview, the status will show “Awaiting User Input”. The status will change to "Success" if draft is successfully saved or Abort if upload draft was discarded.

Downloading File

The data download options for the new Data Management screen were enhanced for the users to be able to have a clear view of what is going to be downloaded. Data download can be performed in different ways for both csv and xlsx files with below field sections:

  1. Complete data set – this option allows the user to download all number of rows considering the applied formatting (columns selection, filtering, and sorting). This option is not limited to the loaded and/or visible data and has the option to enable/disable applied formats.
    • "Apply formatting" checked: this will allow the user to download the dataset including the applied formatting in the screen
    • "Apply formatting" unchecked:  this will allow the user to download the whole dataset and will disregard the applied formatting in the screen
  2. Visible data set – this option allows the user to download only all loaded visible data shown in the screen. This is also the only download option available when Pivot Mode is enabled.
    Spoiler
    The dataset in the Data Management screen is being loaded by batches of 500 rows.
    Example: 
  3. Tooltip messages – hovering over these tooltip messages will give the user an information about Target download options
  4. Download CSV – this option will allow the user to download the file in CSV format
  5. Download XLSX – this option will allow the user to download the file in XLSX format

And that’s it! That concludes my blog post. I hope you enjoy the new features of the new Data Management screen and be able to help you work efficiently in managing your data.

To always be in the loop for more exciting news, please follow and check the tag PaPMCloudSM.

entry.title: Unlocking Data Accessibility: How Accenture Leverages Generative AI RAG for Finance Teams
entry.content:
The Challenge

Finance teams need quick access to crucial information on best practices, accounting guidelines, compliance procedures, and policy requirements. However, this information is often scattered across various platforms and formats, making retrieval difficult, especially under time constraints.

The challenge of navigating this complex landscape can lead to slower decision-making and inconsistencies in global policy application. As teams grow and technologies evolve, ensuring that everyone has the latest material can become a burden on experienced team members.

A streamlined, accessible, and consistently updated knowledge base is needed to empower finance team members. A solution that consolidates information and enhances accessibility through intuitive, everyday language searches would alleviate information overload and enable efficient, data-driven decision-making across Accenture's global finance operations.

 

The Solution

Accenture has implemented an innovative pilot solution leveraging Generative AI, specifically Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology. This advanced approach integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with information retrieval systems, enabling finance teams to access and interact with vast amounts of data using natural, everyday business language.

At the core of this solution is the utilization of vector embeddings and similarity search within a vector database. By employing chunking strategies, large documents and datasets are broken down into manageable pieces, or "chunks." Each chunk is then converted into a high-dimensional vector using embedding functions, which preserve the semantic similarity of the content. These vectors are stored and managed within the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine, which supports create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations using SQL.

When a user inputs a query, the system converts this query into a vector representation. Through similarity search, the system compares this query vector against the stored vectors to find the most relevant chunks of information. This process enables the retrieval of highly pertinent data from across the knowledge base, which the LLM then incorporates to generate accurate and contextually enriched responses.

Key Features of the Solution:

  • Semantic Search and Similarity Matching: By understanding the semantic meaning behind queries and documents, the system can retrieve information that is contextually relevant, even if the exact keywords do not match.
  • Natural Language Processing (NLP): Users can interact with the system using natural language, making the search process intuitive and user-friendly.
  • Consistency and Up-to-Date Guidance: The solution taps into an agreed-upon "best practice knowledge and rules database," ensuring that the guidance provided is consistent and reflects the most current policies and procedures.
  • Empowering New-Joiners & Experts Alike: By providing quick and accurate answers, the tool empowers users to be more self-sufficient, reducing the need for constant support from seasoned Finance professionals.
  •  Enhanced Onboarding Experience: New team members access information seamlessly, facilitating quicker integration and contribution to finance operations.

To consolidate disparate information sources and enhance accessibility through intuitive search capabilities using everyday business language, Accenture explored and piloted an innovative solution utilizing SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Database (Vector DB) offering.

 

 



Exploring the SAP HANA Vector DB Offering

At the heart of Accenture's solution lies the integration of Generative AI with advanced data retrieval systems. Specifically, the team leveraged Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology, which combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with information retrieval mechanisms. This integration enables finance teams to interact with vast amounts of data using natural language, making the search process more intuitive and efficient.

The SAP HANA Cloud Vector DB plays a crucial role in this setup. By utilizing vector embeddings and similarity search capabilities, the system transforms large documents and datasets into high-dimensional vectors that capture the semantic essence of the content. This process involves:

  1. Chunking: Breaking down large documents into manageable pieces or "chunks" to facilitate efficient processing. In our case the chunk size of 1000 words was found to be effective. You may need to experiment a bit to find the size that works for you.
  2. Embedding: Useinf the ADA models from openai we converted each chunk into a numerical vector representation using embedding models that understand semantic relationships.
  3. Vector Storage: SAP HANA Cloud Vector DB was leveraged, which supports comprehensive create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations through SQL
  4. Retrieval: This is where similarity search plays an important role, we needed to use a reranking step before the final results were generated for the user.

Key Technical Considerations

When designing this solution, these were a few critical factors to work through:

  • Security: It was an important requirement to maintain security between the documents of different teams which is established by ensuring different vector tables for different teams.
  • Vector DB – Auto Updates: Automated chunking process and update of Vector DB tables based on user uploaded files on SharePoint. No IT administration is needed, and the business team is directly responsible for maintaining the document store through this process.
  • Authorization: The system validates if a user belongs to a specific team and ensures only the vector tables which contains documents for that specific team is used for retrieval

Responsible AI, Data Privacy and Security are at the core of how Accenture designs Generative AI solutions. Ensuring that sensitive financial data remains secure within the vector embeddings that are required robust access control mechanisms.


Conclusion

Accenture's innovative Generative AI solution with Retrieval-Augmented Generation revolutionizes financial data access by enabling intuitive and efficient natural language searches. By integrating SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Database, which uses vector embeddings and similarity search, the system ensures secure, team-specific document retrieval.

This approach not only enhances internal processes but also reduces reliance on specialized support, promoting efficiency and consistency across global finance operations. With a focus on responsible AI, Accenture ensures robust data privacy and security, making their solution both powerful and trustworthy.
